body: I almost skipped my first internship application because I only met half the requirements. Apply anyway. Let them decide — not your fear.
author: Recent fintech graduate

body: The first time I negotiated salary I was terrified. I used a simple script and got $5k more. The worst they can say is no — and they rarely do.
author: Software engineer, 3 years out

body: Open a Roth IRA with your first internship paycheck. Even $50/month starting at 20 becomes something extraordinary at 60. Future you will thank you.
author: Investment analyst

body: Your network is not about knowing the 'right' people. It's about being genuinely curious about the person in front of you. Start there.
author: Product manager at a top-10 bank

body: I grew up without money conversations in my home. I had to learn everything on my own. That's why I'm here — so you don't have to figure it out alone.
author: First-gen financial advisor

body: Asking 'what's the comp range?' is not awkward. It is professional. Every recruiter expects the question. Write it down and practice saying it out loud.
author: Tech recruiter, 6 years experience

body: The emergency fund felt impossible on a $10/hr campus job. I saved $10/week. It took a year to hit $500. That $500 saved me when my car broke down junior year.
author: Business operations lead

body: You don't need a perfect resume to apply. You need a good-enough resume sent to 20 companies. Volume beats perfection at the internship stage.
author: Data scientist, 2 years out

body: Being the only woman in the room used to make me shrink. Now I treat it as information — this company needs more of us. I decide if I want to be the one.
author: Engineering director

body: One of my biggest money regrets was not tracking my spending for two years. Not to restrict myself — just to know. Knowledge is the starting point.
author: CPA and women's finance coach

body: Internship income planning changed everything for me. I treated my summer earnings like a mini-budget: savings first, then everything else.
author: Finance major turned analyst

body: LinkedIn is not just for job hunting. It's a research tool. Look up where women from your school ended up. Reach out to three of them this month.
author: Consulting recruiter
